surface.name: Render and interact with a Mapbox GL JS map using the CDN route
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Render and interact with a Mapbox GL JS map using the CDN route. Mapbox offers several products and platform routes, but its official GL JS CDN quickstart documents a self-contained web candidate from account token access through an interactive Mapbox Standard map. Prior surface before policy commit: Mapbox product and application platform not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Mapbox GL JS using CDN
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the shortest documented standalone web candidate, but web is only one of the three platforms offered by the Map Developer Journey.

alternatives_considered.name: Maps SDK for iOS
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires Xcode, an iOS app, SwiftUI or UIKit, an iOS dependency method, and simulator selection.

alternatives_considered.name: Maps SDK for Android
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires Android Studio, Gradle, an Empty Activity project, Android resource and Maven configuration, and an Android runtime target.

alternatives_considered.name: Search, Navigation, Location AI, or data API result
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These product families have different SDKs, credentials, requests, UI components, and completion signals from a rendered map.

entry_point.developer_intent: Reach a first Mapbox result without a selected product family or web, iOS, or Android application platform.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Mapbox product family, application platform, dependency method, framework, or target project has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Get up and running with your first map in a few minutes
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Select Maps, Search, Location AI, Navigation, or data products; for the documented Maps journey, select web, iOS, or Android and an installation variant.
Create or sign into a Mapbox account; the account provides a default public access token.
Use a valid public access token from the Mapbox Developer Console in the selected application's documented location.
The selected platform determines the local toolchain: a web project and browser, Xcode and an iOS project, or Android Studio, Gradle, and an Android project.
Package or CDN resources, map styles, tiles, and other Mapbox resources require network access at first render.

primary_path.action: Choose the Mapbox product result and, for the official first-map journey, select web, iOS, or Android plus the platform's installation and framework variant.
details: The Getting Started overview includes Maps, Search, Location AI, Navigation, and data products.
The Add a map stage supplies separate web, iOS, and Android guides; GL JS further offers CDN, npm, and scaffolded-project routes.
primary_path.input: Application type, existing codebase, language and framework, target devices, and desired location capability.
primary_path.output: A selected official Mapbox Get Started guide and implementation variant.
primary_path.success_signal: No shared application path exists at the router, though the first-map outcome is named.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The rest cannot be represented as an atomic page sequence until the platform is selected.

primary_path.action: Create or sign into a Mapbox account, retrieve its default public access token, and enter the selected platform guide.
details: The Map Developer Journey states that signup automatically creates an access token.
The Developer Console manages tokens, usage, billing, styles, datasets, and Studio.
primary_path.input: Email or existing account and selected application platform.
primary_path.output: Mapbox account session, default public token, and chosen guide.
primary_path.success_signal: A valid `pk.` public token is available for the application.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup, sign-in, email verification, account deactivation, token restrictions, or billing configuration can prevent authenticated resource access; exact signup pages are not publicly enumerated in the docs.

primary_path.action: Follow the chosen web, iOS, or Android guide through dependency setup, token configuration, map-container or view code, application run, and interactive-map verification.
details: Each guide uses Mapbox Standard style and ends with an interactable map rendered in the selected runtime.
Adding custom data, interactions, and deployment are later stages of the Map Developer Journey.
primary_path.input: Selected platform, valid token, local project, and toolchain.
primary_path.output: Interactive Mapbox map in a browser, iOS simulator, or Android runtime.
primary_path.success_signal: A map is visibly rendered and responds to drag and zoom interaction in the selected application.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid token, absent container dimensions, dependency or repository failure, build configuration, unsupported runtime, network resource failure, or wrong simulator or device selection can block rendering.

primary_path.action: Create or sign into a Mapbox account.
details: The Map Developer Journey states that account signup automatically creates an access token.
primary_path.input: Mapbox account identity and signup or sign-in details.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can access the Mapbox account and Developer Console.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup, email verification, sign-in, or account-status failure prevents token access.

primary_path.action: Locate and copy the automatically created default public access token.
details: The web quickstart requires a public token with a `pk.` prefix.
primary_path.success_signal: A valid public token is available for the page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing, deactivated, invalid, or improperly restricted token prevents map resources from loading.

primary_path.action: Create or open a standalone HTML file for the map.
primary_path.input: Writable local project folder and text editor.
primary_path.success_signal: The document can be saved and opened or served in a browser.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing document or unwritable location stops page construction.

primary_path.action: Add the current Mapbox GL JS stylesheet link and JavaScript script from the documented CDN to the HTML head.
details: This route loads GL JS directly from the CDN and does not require npm.
primary_path.input: HTML document and current CDN resource URLs from the guide.
primary_path.output: Page with Mapbox GL JS CSS and JavaScript dependencies.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser can request both documented resources.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incorrect versions, blocked CDN access, or missing CSS or script prevents correct rendering.

primary_path.action: Add the map container `div` and give it nonzero width and height.
details: The quickstart troubleshooting guidance identifies missing container dimensions as a cause of a blank map.
primary_path.success_signal: The container occupies visible page space.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A zero-height or missing container leaves no visible surface for the map.

primary_path.action: Set `mapboxgl.accessToken` to the public token and instantiate `mapboxgl.Map` with the container, starting center, and zoom from the guide.
details: The documented working example renders the Mapbox Standard style.
primary_path.input: Public token, dimensioned container, center, and zoom values.
primary_path.output: Configured Mapbox GL JS map instance.
primary_path.success_signal: The page JavaScript runs without a syntax or token configuration error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Placeholder token, invalid token, wrong container ID, syntax, or configuration errors prevent initialization.

primary_path.action: Save and open or locally serve the HTML page in a browser.
primary_path.input: Completed HTML page and browser network access.
primary_path.output: Loaded web page requesting Mapbox resources.
primary_path.success_signal: The Mapbox Standard map becomes visible in the container.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the map is blank, check token replacement and validity, container dimensions, network requests, and browser-console errors before retrying.

primary_path.action: Drag and zoom the rendered map to verify that it is interactive.
details: Adding custom data, application interactions, and deployment are later stages of the Map Developer Journey.
primary_path.output: Rendered and interactable first web map.
primary_path.success_signal: The map visibly responds to drag and zoom input.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A static, blank, or erroring surface means the documented first-map result has not been reached.

branches.condition: Standalone web with the documented CDN route is selected.
branches.path: Create or open an HTML file, add the current Mapbox GL JS CSS `<link>` and JavaScript `<script>` CDN resources to the document head, add a `div` map container with nonzero width and height, instantiate `mapboxgl.Map` with the public access token, container ID, starting center and zoom, open or serve the page, then verify that the Standard map appears and supports drag and zoom. If blank, confirm token replacement and validity, dimensions, and browser-console errors, then retry.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The browser displays an interactive Mapbox map; no npm package or mobile build is required.

branches.condition: Web with npm or an existing JavaScript framework is selected.
branches.path: Run `npm install mapbox-gl`, import the library and its CSS in the framework-specific form, create a dimensioned map container, instantiate the Map with the public token, center and zoom in the correct lifecycle hook, build and run the project, then verify the rendered interactive map. React, Svelte, Vue, and plain JavaScript have separate examples; the scaffold alternative runs `npm create @mapbox/web-app`, answers the framework prompts, and ends with a generated project that renders a map.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Package manager, framework lifecycle, build command, and cleanup logic are added to the web route.

branches.path: Install Xcode, open or create an iOS App project, add `MBXAccessToken` to `Info.plist` with the default public token, choose Swift Package Manager as the recommended dependency route or another documented method, add `mapbox-maps-ios`, select the project target, wait for MapboxCommon, MapboxCoreMaps, MapboxMaps, and Turf to install, ensure MapboxMaps is linked, replace the SwiftUI `ContentView` or UIKit `ViewController` with the guide code, select a specific iOS simulator such as iPhone 16, run the app, and verify an interactable map. If absent, verify token, linked libraries, embed settings, and simulator target before retrying.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Xcode project, package-resolution wait, iOS code branch, build, and simulator gates replace the web steps.

branches.path: Install the current Android Studio and Gradle environment, create an Empty Activity project, add the public token as the `mapbox_access_token` string resource, optionally skip location permissions for the first default map, add the Mapbox Maven repository under dependency resolution, add the current Maps SDK dependency and any required Compose configuration, sync Gradle, add the documented map view or Compose code, build and run on an Android emulator or device, and verify the initialized interactive map.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Android resource, Gradle repository, dependency, optional permissions, UI framework, build, and emulator or device gates replace the other paths.

branches.condition: Search, Navigation, Location AI, or a data API is selected instead of Maps.
branches.path: Leave the Map Developer Journey and use the selected product's official overview and Get Started guide to define its SDK or API request, credential scope, inputs, execution, and product-specific result.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The interactive-map milestone no longer applies; a search result, route or navigation session, AI result, or data response becomes the candidate outcome.

friction_gates.description: Product family, application platform, dependency method, framework, and target runtime must be selected.
A Mapbox account is required and automatically supplies the default public token.
Every selected Maps SDK route places a valid public token into code or platform configuration.

The Getting Started page states that most products begin with free tiers and become metered; billing settings are managed in the Developer Console, but the guides checked do not document a universal card-entry step before the first map.
CDN resources, npm packages, Swift packages, or Android Maven dependencies must load for the selected platform.
Container dimensions, project resource files, package targets, repositories, dependencies, and runtime targets vary by platform.
Package resolution, compilation, simulator or emulator start, and map-resource loading must finish before the map is visible.
The visible map must load and respond to interaction; a successful build without rendered map resources is not the documented milestone.

time_to_first_success.value: a few minutes for the first map

sources.title: Add a map
evidence_supported: Standard style and Hello world positioning

sources.title: Getting Started with Mapbox GL JS
Get started with Mapbox GL JS using a CDN
Get Started with Maps SDK for iOS
Get Started with the Maps SDK for Android
evidence_supported: Android Studio, Gradle, and project prerequisites

uncertainties.question: Which Mapbox product family and, for Maps, which application platform and dependency variant should define first success?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes toolchain, credential placement, code, package manager, runtime, waits, troubleshooting, and the observable result.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official Mapbox router establishes interactive mapping as the most common workflow but explicitly requires web, iOS, or Android selection and presents other product families alongside Maps.

uncertainties.question: Does every new-account workshop participant encounter a billing or card page before the first map?
uncertainties.impact: A mandatory payment-method page would add a high-friction gate to the account sequence.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official public docs describe free tiers, metering, and console billing settings but do not publish a stable signup-screen transcript or universal pre-first-map card requirement; live signup is prohibited.

excluded_after_success: Navigation, Search, Location AI, and data products unless chosen as the primary surface
Production token restriction, observability, and cost controls
